title: ASM Trust Delta receipt envelope v0.1
description: Envelope for delivering Trust Delta receipts from a publisher to a consumer. Push (POST to consumer's payment.receipt_endpoint) and poll (consumer GETs publisher's .well-known/asm-receipts) both use this envelope. Specifies the v0.1 receipt body shape, signature requirements, retry semantics, and the supersedes correction protocol. See RFC: docs/rfcs/trust-delta-receipt-extension-v0.1.md.

envelope_version.description: Envelope version. Pinned to '0.1' for this schema; later versions ship their own schema files.

publisher.description: Stable identifier for the receipt-emitting organisation. By convention the org prefix of the service_id (e.g. 'akkhar-labs').

service_id.description: ASM service_id of the receipt emitter. MUST match the service_id field inside every receipt in this envelope.

issued_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp when this envelope was emitted (not when individual receipts were generated).

receipts.description: One or more receipt bodies. Multi-receipt batches are allowed for poll-mode efficiency; each receipt is independently verifiable.

retry_advisory.description: Optional. Publisher advertises its expected push retry semantics so consumers can size their dead-letter handling accordingly. Advisory in v0.1; enforced in v0.2.

receipt_version.description: Semver of the receipt body shape. Pinned to '0.1' for this envelope version.

pipeline_id.description: Opaque identifier unique per invocation. Doubles as the idempotency key — consumers MUST treat repeated receipts with the same pipeline_id as duplicates of the original (use 'supersedes' for explicit corrections).

supersedes.description: If present, this receipt corrects a previously-emitted receipt with the named pipeline_id. The original is not mutated; Trust Delta MUST record the correction as an explicit signal rather than a silent overwrite.

reason.description: Optional human-readable explanation for a 'supersedes' correction. Recommended when supersedes is set.

service_id.description: ASM service_id of the emitter. MUST match the envelope-level service_id.

delegates_to.description: Optional attribution chain for receipts emitted by pipelines that wrap sub-services. v0.1 minimum: pipeline-level seal sufficient. v0.2 (future): each delegate carries its own provider-signed receipt for end-to-end verification.

extensions.description: Service-specific fields, keyed by the publisher's service_id. Consumers MUST ignore unknown extension keys.

scope.description: Identifies what the seal covers (e.g. 'execution_payload'). The byte-range construction is service-specific and documented in the publisher's integration spec.

digest.description: Hex SHA-256 digest prefixed with 'sha256:'. Covers the canonical bytes described by 'scope'.

byte_range_spec.description: Optional URL or anchor pointing at the publisher's documented seal construction. Lets verifiers recompute the seal offline.

dimension.description: Primary billing dimension (e.g. 'pipeline_run', 'input_token', 'output_token'). MUST appear in the corresponding ASM manifest's pricing.billing_dimensions.

cost.description: Publisher's settlement claim. Trust Delta recomputes from observed_input_tokens + observed_output_tokens against manifest pricing and surfaces the delta as a first-class trust signal.

observed_input_tokens.description: Aggregate input tokens across all phases. Used for cost re-derivation.

phase.description: Free-form label identifying which phase/step this delegate served.

asm_service_id.description: ASM service_id of the upstream provider this delegate represents.

model_id.description: Scoped identifier the parent service used internally (e.g. 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4@4.0:phase2-planner').

upstream_receipt_id.description: Optional reference to a separately-issued ASM receipt from the upstream provider.

upstream_seal_digest.description: Optional sha256 of the upstream provider's own seal, if it emitted one.

algorithm.description: Pinned to Ed25519 in v0.1. Pluggable algorithm list deferred to v0.2.

public_key_url.description: Stable URL hosting the Ed25519 public key (PEM format). Consumers cache and refresh per the URL's HTTP cache headers.

public_key_fingerprint.description: Optional in v0.1 (RECOMMENDED), REQUIRED in v0.2. SHA-256 of the DER-encoded public key. Lets consumers detect key substitution at the public_key_url between fetches without re-downloading every time.

value.description: Hex-encoded Ed25519 signature over canonical_json(envelope minus this signature.value field).

retry_advisory.description: Advisory retry semantics for push delivery. Documented v0.1 defaults: 1s initial delay, 2x backoff, 5 retries (~31s window), dead-letter after 6 hours. Consumers MAY advertise different values via this object.

recommended_max_bytes.note: Advisory in v0.1 (not schema-enforced). Enforced in v0.2 once real payload distributions are observed.
